Bio notes: He was born John Mitropolous, in the Community of Kakourekia, Greece, son of Dimitrios and Sophia (Gianoukos), and is enrolled in the archives of mail births in Archive No. 2 with a year of birth 1894.  He probably immigrated to this country in the early 20th Century (probably 1906-08).  He arrived in New York and lived with his cousins-in-law in Hartford, Ct.  where he worked at various jobs...mostly in the food business with his cousins.  His dream was to come to this country, work hard and make a new life filled with the opportunities he heard about while a boy in the Peloponises (sp.) Mountains, Greece.  He saved his money and opened a restaurant of his own in Hartford and sent for his brother, Nicholas, who left his wife and family in Greece.  As times were tough, he needed the money.  He did not have the dedication to this country that my Dad had and he went back to Greece with a considerable amount of money.  Being adventurous Dad traveled to 21 states before he married my mother.  After I was born in 1931, my parents opened a restaurant - the St. James - on Euclid Avenue, Cleveland, OH.   I'm not sure about the restaurants he had or their names and dates; but I know he had one in Hartford, Kansas City, MO, and one in Phila. at 5th & Olney Avenue.  This is where he met my mother who came to the restaurant on a date.  He was smitten at once.  In about 1936-37, he opened a restaurant at 30th & Lancaster Avenue, Phila. with his partner Johnny Papas and together they operated the restaurant for about 30 years.  Dad and my mother retired to Fort Lauderdale, FL.  When the Ellis Island Foundation opened their Wall of Honor, I enrolled my Dad (John Mitropoulos).  My father was the most wonderful, caring,

Bio notes: Born on Aug 23 1910 in Shenandoah, PA. Lived in Shenandoah Ward 3, Schuylkill Co., PA in 1920 and moved to Philadelphia in 1930. Got married in 1934 to Ryde Shryock Stewart. Had his first and only son, Robert Cosgrove in 1937. He later died probably still in Philadelphia on Nov 19th, 1989.

Merchant Marine WWII — highest per-capita casualty rate of any US service (1 in 26 killed, 733 ships sunk). Denied veteran status until Jan 19, 1988 (Schumacher v. Aldridge). Died Nov 19, 1989 — only 22 months after recognition. Service records at NARA Record Group 26, St. Louis (opened Dec 2019).

notes: Bio notes: Henry Shryock was an officer in the Revolutionary War. He was Lieutenant Colonel of the Sixth Battalion, under Colonel Otho Holland Williams. See McSherry's History of Maryland; page 382, Appendix B, and page 387, Appendix C.
Sheriff Washington County 1789-92, MD House of Delegates 1788-89. Signer of MD ratification of Constitution April 28, 1788. Co-signed welcome address to President Washington Oct 1790.

CORRECTION: Previous parents "Friedrick Von Schreyack" and "Hildergarde Von Schreyack" were FABRICATED by Frank Kimball Leland (~1930s). Correct father: Jacob Schreyack (b.1714). Correct mother: Barbara Wolf. NOTE: The existing GEDCOM incorrectly lists Jacob Schreyack as Catherine's father — he was actually HENRY's father (Schreyack→Shryock name evolution). Catherine's maiden name was Soloday.

Bio notes: When James J. Cosgrove was born in 1841, his father, Patrick, was 22 and his mother, Ellen, was 21. He married Winifred Gibbons in September 1870 in Shuylkill, PA. They had nine children in 16 years. He died on December 21, 1891, in Shenandoah, PA, at the age of 50, and was buried there.

Birthplace "Cork, Buttevant Ireland" — likely Cavan-to-Cork via Buttevant military barracks. Listed in 1881 Munsell History of Schuylkill County under "Regiments of Numbers Not Known". Absent from T288 pension index and 1883 pension roll — likely served <90 days in 1863 emergency militia.

Bio notes: Winifred Gibbons was born on May 3, 1849, in Ireland, the daughter of Katherine and Peter. She married James J. Cosgrove in September 1870 in Schuylkill PA. They had nine children in 16 years. She died on March 18, 1933, in Shenandoah, PA, at the age of 83, and was buried there.

Bio notes: When Ella Cosgrove was born in 1870 in Shenandoah, PA, her father, James, was 29 and her mother Winifred, was 21. She ha 8 brothers. She died as a teenager on September 6, 1884, in her hometown.

Bio notes: When John Francis Cosgrove was born on June 19, 1883. in Shenandoah, PA, his father, James, was 32, and his mother, Winifred, was 24. He had 7 brothers and one sister. He married Sophia Barth on June 7th, 1906, in Delaware, and had one kid during their marriage. He went to fight in the Spanish American War and served in the military on April 1, 1901, in San Francisco, CA, when he was 27 years old. He died on June 14, 1933, in Philadelphia, PA, at the age of 59.
Birth year confirmed as 1873 (not 1883). Spanish-American War service — stationed San Francisco/Angel Island 1901 at age 27.

Bio notes: When Patrick Henry Cosgrove was born on May 3, 1877, in Shenandoah, PA, his father, James, was 36 and his mother, Winifred, was 28. He had seven brothers and one sister. He died on December 7, 1955, in his hometown at the age of 78, and was also buried there.

Bio notes: When Thomas Edward Cosgrove was born on June 24, 1877, in Shenandoah, PA, his father, James, was 36 and his mother, Winifred, was 28. He had seven brothers and one sister. He died on May 9, 1899, in his hometown at the age of 21.

Bio notes: When James Fitzgerald Cosgrove was born on May 6, 1879, in Shenandoah, PA, his father, Jaemes, was 38, and his mother, Winifred, was 30. He had seven brothers and one sister. He died as a child in July 1880 in his hometown.

Bio notes: When William James Cosgrove was born on April 16, 1880, in Shenandoah, PA, his father, James, was 39 and his mother, inifred, was 30. He married Anna King in 1906. They had six children in 10 years. He died on November 20, 1976, in his hometown at the age of 96, and was buried there.
Lived to age 96, apparently never married, no known children. Private, stationed Angel Island, CA, Mar 22, 1902 (Spanish-American War).

Bio notes: When Charles Cosgove was born on May 24, 1884, in Shenandoah, PA, his father, James, was 43, and his mother, Winifred, was 35. He had seven brothers and one sister. He died on June 10, 1917, in Philadelphia, PA, at the age of 33.

Bio notes: When Martin Cosgrove was born on April 8, 1886, in Shenandoah, PA, his father, James, was 45, and his mother, Winifred, was 36. He had seven brothers and one sister. He died on August 5, 1886, in his hometown within a year of his birth.

Bio notes: When Patrick Cosgrove was born in 1819 in Cavan, Ireland, his father, John, was 39. He married Ellen Fitzgerald in 1840 in Ireland. They had five children in 19 years. He moved to Philadelphia, PA, on April 15, 1847. He died on November 24, 1873, at the age of 54, and was buried at Saint Vincent de Paul Cemetery #1 in Minersville, PA.

Arrived Philadelphia April 15, 1847 (during Great Famine). Ship manifest likely on NARA Microfilm M425, Reel 64. Settled in Minersville, PA. Died Nov 24, 1873. Buried Saint Vincent de Paul Cemetery #1. NOT killed by Molly Maguires — likely died from cumulative mining work effects at age 54.
Bio notes: Picture of Mary and her granddaughter Mary Sheridan.

Bio notes: When Ellen Cosgrove was born in December 1856 in Somerset, PA, her father, Patrick, was 37, and her mother, Ellen, was 36. She had one son and five daughters with Hohn F. Reynolds between 1879 and 1888. She died in 1918 at the age of 62.

Bio notes: James John Stanton was born in 1845. He married Mary Cosgrove in 1857 in Aghada, Cork, Ireland. They had 14 children in 23 years. He moved around a lot. He was born in Mayo, Ireland, and got married there too. He later lived in Manchester, Lancashire, England, in 1961. Then, he moved back to Ireland to have three of his children in Ireland. In 1871, he was recorded living in Govan, Lanarksshire, Scotland. Came back to Shanandoah in 1874 and owned a piece of land  in Shenandoah; Mahanoy, Schuylkill, PA. He died on December 27, 1891, in Shenandoah, PA, when he was 46 years old.
Bio notes: Picture of her and her father, James John Stanton

Bio notes: When John F Reynolds was born on August 15, 1854, his father, Thomas, was 42 and his mother, Mar, was 36. He had one son and five daughters with Ellen Cosgrove between 1879 and 1888. He died on April 7, 1899, in Mahanomy Plane, PA, at the age of 44.
